rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2026. Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., NEILL BASSI Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination is continued to May 1, 2026 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Order (Ccp Section 663) Sustaining Demurrer To Eighth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2026. Line 2. PLAINTIFF MIHAL EMBERTON Motion To Vacate Order (Ccp Section 663) Sustaining Demurrer To Eighth Amended Complaint is DENIED. As Plaintiff points out, there was no trial in this action. CCP 663 applies to judgments or decrees after trial. Although the City's opposition is based solely on the procedural impropriety of Plaintiff's motion under CCP 663, Plaintiff's reply contains no discussion whatsoever of how she can obtain relief under CCP 663. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Deem Plaintiff Sarah Lewis A Vexatious Litigant By Defendant Arthur Traynor
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2026. Line 3. DEFENDANT ARTHUR TRAYNOR Notice Of Motion To Deem Plaintiff Sarah Lewis A Vexatious Litigant is DENIED. Oversized Memorandum of Points and Authorities is stricken. As such, motion is not supported by authorities. CRC 3.1113(d). The parties shall also familiarize themselves with other rules of court, including, but not limited to CRC 2.104 and 2.105. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 915 FOLSOM STREET, LLC VS. CALVIN BREEDLOVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 23, 2026. Line 17. DEFENDANT CALVIN BREEDLOVE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is MOOT. Prior action has been dismissed. Defendant to file an answer within 5 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
